situation: audit-2026-07-08-035 H-COVERAGE-001: 17 non-test .lock().unwrap() calls remain across 5 crates after b877104/2e28a5f fixed moni-strategy. factor-registry (crate 1/5, 6 sites) done in case-103. This case covers moni-replay (5 sites in lib.rs). Remaining: moni-post-close(2), moni-qlib-adapter(2), moni-core config.rs(4).
action: Replace 5 .lock().unwrap() with .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) in ReplayStore impl: list_sessions(L76-77), get_session(L87), get_step(L91), get_all_steps(L103), create_session(L235). Same pattern as b877104. cargo check -p moni-replay passed. Committed directly in worktree (case-104 escape hatch: cp-guard triggered due to unmerged factor-registry commit on same branch).
outcome_evidence: commit 7581bc1 on auto/opt-moni-master-1783105913 pushed to origin; cargo check -p moni-replay clean; grep confirms 0 remaining .lock().unwrap() in moni-replay/src/lib.rs (5 sites converted to unwrap_or_else)
next_suggestion: H-COVERAGE-001 crate 3/5: moni-post-close (2 sites in lib.rs:39,73). Then qlib-adapter(2), core config.rs(4). After all 5 crates dispatched+merged, audit-cycle-state → cycle-complete.

audit_findings.finding: 5 .lock().unwrap() calls in ReplayStore — same cascade-panic-on-poison vulnerability class as b877104 fixed in moni-strategy. If any thread panics while holding sessions Mutex, all subsequent callers panic.
audit_findings.remediation: Replace with .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) to recover poisoned lock and continue serving replay data.
